---
title: "Yield response to sulfur fertilizer and sulfur-based nutrient co-application in crop production"
subtitle: "A systematic review and meta-analysis"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
if (basename(getwd()) == "manuscript") {
  setwd("..")
}
source("analysis/run_pipeline.R")
effects <- readr::read_csv("data/processed/effect_sizes.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
overall <- readr::read_csv("data/processed/overall.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
```

# Abstract

## Background

Sulfur deficiency can limit crop production, yet sulfur is frequently applied with other nutrients. This study estimates yield responses to sulfur fertilization while separating sulfur-only effects from sulfur co-application effects.

## Methods

We extracted treatment-control contrasts from agronomic experiments and prioritized comparisons where sulfur was the only difference between treatment and control. The primary effect size was the log response ratio of crop yield.

## Results

The current repository includes a synthetic demonstration dataset. Replace it with extracted studies before interpreting results.

## Conclusions

Background-matched contrasts provide a more defensible estimate of sulfur fertilizer effects than broad fertilizer-package comparisons.

# Introduction

Sulfur is essential for amino acid synthesis, protein formation, enzyme activity, and crop quality. Modern fertilization systems may create sulfur limitations through higher crop nutrient removal and reduced incidental sulfur inputs. However, agronomic sulfur trials often combine sulfur with other nutrients, making it difficult to isolate the sulfur contribution.

# Methods

## Eligibility criteria

See `protocol/protocol.md` and `docs/screening_rules.md`.

## Effect size

The primary effect size was:

```text
lnRR = log(mean yield with S / mean yield without S)
```

## Statistical analysis

Random-effects and multilevel meta-analytic models were fitted using `metafor`.

# Results

```{r}
knitr::kable(overall, digits = 3)
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/yield_gain_by_role.png")
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/dose_response.png")
```

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/soil_s_gradient.png")
```

# Discussion

The final discussion should interpret sulfur response by crop sulfur demand, soil sulfur status, source, rate, and co-applied nutrient background.

# Reproducibility

All code, templates, and synthetic demo data are available in this repository.
